resources.string: This demo shows the difference between a regular screen and a hidden mode that blocks the accessibility tree, sets FLAG_SECURE, and marks the root View as sensitive where supported.
Mode is off: the accessibility service can read text and field values.
Mode is on: secure-ui blocks the tree and hides content from the accessibility service on a best-effort basis.
Install the attacker module and enable its Accessibility Service in settings.
Compose secret: confirmation code 517 204 and backup phrase orbit stone maple.
The `secure-ui` library has hidden the accessibility tree and added extra best-effort flags. An external Accessibility Service should no longer receive sensitive content from this screen.
XML: access token A1B2-C3D4-E5F6 and internal PIN 7788.
The screen is currently visible to accessibility.
The screen is currently hidden from accessibility by a best-effort policy.
